main_paper.raw: {"methods":["PepHAR: a hot-spot-driven autoregressive generative model for peptide design","Energy-based density model for hot spot residue identification and sampling using Langevin dynamics","Autoregressive fragment extension by modeling dihedral angles with a von Mises distribution to maintain peptide bond geometry","Hybrid optimization function for fragment assembly to ensure correct peptide structures"],"problems":["Inefficient peptide binder design due to treating all residues equally, despite only some (hot spots) being critical for binding","Difficulty in generating peptides that respect geometric constraints imposed by peptide bonds","Lack of realistic in-silico benchmarks for peptide drug discovery, such as scaffold generation where known hot spots are linked by scaffolds"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of PepHAR, a generative model that explicitly distinguishes between hot spot and scaffold residues in peptide design","Use of an energy-based model to identify and sample hot spot residues, rather than generating all residues simultaneously","Autoregressive fragment extension that models dihedral angles to maintain peptide bond geometry, improving structural validity","A new experimental setting (scaffold generation) that mimics practical peptide drug discovery scenarios, enabling the model to scaffold known hot spot residues into functional peptides"]}

methods: PepHAR: a hot-spot-driven autoregressive generative model for peptide design
Energy-based density model for hot spot residue identification and sampling using Langevin dynamics
Autoregressive fragment extension by modeling dihedral angles with a von Mises distribution to maintain peptide bond geometry
Hybrid optimization function for fragment assembly to ensure correct peptide structures
problems: Inefficient peptide binder design due to treating all residues equally, despite only some (hot spots) being critical for binding
Difficulty in generating peptides that respect geometric constraints imposed by peptide bonds
Lack of realistic in-silico benchmarks for peptide drug discovery, such as scaffold generation where known hot spots are linked by scaffolds
novelty_claims: Introduction of PepHAR, a generative model that explicitly distinguishes between hot spot and scaffold residues in peptide design
Use of an energy-based model to identify and sample hot spot residues, rather than generating all residues simultaneously
Autoregressive fragment extension that models dihedral angles to maintain peptide bond geometry, improving structural validity
A new experimental setting (scaffold generation) that mimics practical peptide drug discovery scenarios, enabling the model to scaffold known hot spot residues into functional peptides

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["HelixGAN: a generative adversarial network (GAN) for de novo generation of left-handed and right-handed alpha-helix structures at atomic level","Gradient-based search in latent space to optimize generated helices to match conformations of selected hotspot residues","Comparison with PEP-FOLD (de novo peptide structure prediction)","Comparison with RosettaDesign (peptide design tool)","Comparison with a previously developed structural similarity method for D-peptide generation"],"problems":["De novo design of alpha-helix structures at atomic resolution","Designing helices that match specific hotspot conformations for binding or biological activity","Generating D-peptides that match hotspots of known L-peptides","Improving stability and binding of designed peptides for therapeutic applications"],"datasets":["Not explicitly stated; novelty_claims: FlowSite is the first deep learning solution to design binding sites for small molecules without prior knowledge of the molecule structure.

FlowSite is the first general and automated approach for multi-ligand binding site design, addressing a previously unsolved problem in deep learning.

methods: Iterative Refinement Graph Neural Network (GNN) for co-designing antibody CDR sequence and structure
Graph-based representation of sequence-structure pairs
Autoregressive sequence generation interleaved with iterative global structure refinement
Coarse-grained graph representation by grouping nodes into blocks for efficient context modeling
Pretraining on antibodies with known structures followed by finetuning with property predictors
problems: Automatic design of antibody CDRs with enhanced binding specificity or neutralization capabilities
Joint (co-)design of antibody CDR sequence and 3D structure, rather than assuming structure is given
Modeling conditional dependence between CDRs and the rest of the antibody at both sequence and structure levels
Efficiently propagating contextual information in long antibody sequences for CDR design
Optimizing antibody design for complex properties beyond binding energy
datasets: Antibody structures with known sequences and 3D structures (pretraining set)
SARS-CoV-2 neutralizing antibody dataset (for property-guided design)
metrics: Performance in property-guided antibody design tasks (e.g., SARS-CoV-2 neutralization)

results.value: Superior to previous baselines on the test set
Lower than standard sequence models and state-of-the-art graph generation methods
Outperforms previous baselines in designing SARS-CoV-2 neutralizing antibodies

novelty_claims: First model to co-design antibody CDR sequence and structure as a graph generation problem, rather than assuming structure is given a priori
Introduces an iterative refinement approach to structure prediction interleaved with autoregressive sequence generation, addressing error accumulation in previous autoregressive models
Proposes a coarse-grained graph representation to efficiently model long-range context in antibody sequences
Demonstrates superior performance in both language modeling and property-guided antibody design tasks compared to previous baselines

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Combining Foldseek, ESM-IF1, and AlphaFold2 in a joint framework for peptide binder design","Using Foldseek to generate backbone seeds for peptide design","Adapting ESM-IF1 for protein complex design","Evaluating designed sequences with AlphaFold2 using MSA for receptor and single sequence for binder","Developing a bind score based on AlphaFold2 evaluation"],"problems":["Designing peptide binders that specifically target protein interfaces","Improving computational peptide binder design to outperform or match AlphaFold2 predictions","Selecting successful peptide binders from computational designs"],"datasets":["Heteromeric and homomeric protein interfaces (specific numbers: 185 heteromeric, 42 homomeric interfaces mentioned)"],"metrics":["ROC AUC","plDDT (predicted Local Distance Difference Test)","Interface RMSD (Root Mean Square Deviation)","Sequence recovery in interface positions"],"results":[{"metric":"ROC AUC (heterodimeric case)","value":"0.96"},{"metric":"Average plDDT (≥80% sequence recovery)","value":"84"},{"metric":"Average plDDT (0% sequence recovery)","value":"55"},{"metric":"Median plDDT (designed vs non-intended receptors)","value":"60% higher for intended receptors"},{"metric":"Successful binders (interface RMSD ≤ 2 Å) for heteromeric interfaces (ESM-IF1)","value":"185 (6.5%)"},{"metric":"Successful binders (interface RMSD ≤ 2 Å) for homomeric interfaces (ESM-IF1)","value":"42 (3.6%)"},{"metric":"Successful binders (interface RMSD ≤ 2 Å) for heteromeric interfaces (ProteinMPNN)","value":"18 (1.5%)"}],"novelty_claims":["First joint framework combining Foldseek, ESM-IF1, and AlphaFold2 for peptide binder design","Adaptation of ESM-IF1 for protein complex design","Demonstration that AlphaFold2 can accurately evaluate protein binders and that a bind score can effectively select successful binders","Improved success rate in designing peptide binders compared to previous methods such as ProteinMPNN"]}

methods: Combining Foldseek, ESM-IF1, and AlphaFold2 in a joint framework for peptide binder design
Using Foldseek to generate backbone seeds for peptide design
Adapting ESM-IF1 for protein complex design
Evaluating designed sequences with AlphaFold2 using MSA for receptor and single sequence for binder
Developing a bind score based on AlphaFold2 evaluation
problems: Designing peptide binders that specifically target protein interfaces
Improving computational peptide binder design to outperform or match AlphaFold2 predictions
Selecting successful peptide binders from computational designs
datasets: Heteromeric and homomeric protein interfaces (specific numbers: 185 heteromeric, 42 homomeric interfaces mentioned)

results.value: 60% higher for intended receptors

results.metric: Successful binders (interface RMSD ≤ 2 Å) for heteromeric interfaces (ESM-IF1)
Successful binders (interface RMSD ≤ 2 Å) for homomeric interfaces (ESM-IF1)
Successful binders (interface RMSD ≤ 2 Å) for heteromeric interfaces (ProteinMPNN)

novelty_claims: First joint framework combining Foldseek, ESM-IF1, and AlphaFold2 for peptide binder design
Adaptation of ESM-IF1 for protein complex design
Demonstration that AlphaFold2 can accurately evaluate protein binders and that a bind score can effectively select successful binders
Improved success rate in designing peptide binders compared to previous methods such as ProteinMPNN

methods: Diffusion-based generative model for antibody CDR sequence-structure co-design

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["AbDiffuser: an equivariant and physics-informed diffusion model for joint generation of antibody 3D structures and sequences","Aligned Protein Mixer (APMixer): a universal SE(3) equivariant neural network for modeling residue-to-residue relations and handling sequence length changes","New protein structure representation with projection method to guarantee bond and angle constraints in coordinate space","Incorporation of family-specific priors into the diffusion process"],"problems":["Generation of full-atom antibody structures and sequences that are functional in vitro","Efficient and accurate modeling of antibody sequence and structure, including backbone and side chains","Reducing memory complexity in protein generative models to enable full antibody generation","Improving the quality and efficiency of antibody design for drug discovery"],"datasets":["Paired Observable Antibody Space (pOAS)","HER2 binder datasets (for modeling and experimental validation)"],"metrics":["Expression rate (percentage of generated antibodies expressed in vitro)","Binding rate (percentage of generated antibodies that bind to target)","Average pKD significantly smaller memory footprint.","New protein structure representation and projection method that guarantees bond and angle constraints in coordinate space, enabling efficient diffusion modeling.","Demonstration that incorporating family-specific priors into the diffusion process improves generation efficiency and quality.","Experimental evidence that a generative model trained on methods: AbDiffuser: an equivariant and physics-informed diffusion model for joint generation of antibody 3D structures and sequences
Aligned Protein Mixer (APMixer): a universal SE(3) equivariant neural network for modeling residue-to-residue relations and handling sequence length changes
New protein structure representation with projection method to guarantee bond and angle constraints in coordinate space
Incorporation of family-specific priors into the diffusion process
problems: Generation of full-atom antibody structures and sequences that are functional in vitro
Efficient and accurate modeling of antibody sequence and structure, including backbone and side chains
Reducing memory complexity in protein generative models to enable full antibody generation
Improving the quality and efficiency of antibody design for drug discovery
datasets: Paired Observable Antibody Space (pOAS)
HER2 binder datasets (for modeling and experimental validation)
metrics: Expression rate (percentage of generated antibodies expressed in vitro)
Binding rate (percentage of generated antibodies that bind to target)
Sequence and structural similarity to reference set
novelty_claims: First generative model to jointly generate full-atom antibody structures and sequences that are validated in vitro without post-selection by learned or physics-based binding predictors.
Introduction of the Aligned Protein Mixer (APMixer), a new SE(3) equivariant neural network architecture that handles sequence length changes and has a significantly smaller memory footprint.
New protein structure representation and projection method that guarantees bond and angle constraints in coordinate space, enabling efficient diffusion modeling.
Demonstration that incorporating family-specific priors into the diffusion process improves generation efficiency and quality.
Experimental evidence that a generative model trained on mutagenesis data can reliably create new antibody binders of high affinity.

novelty_claims: First all-atom diffusion model for protein structure that superposes possible sidechain states and collapses them for reverse diffusion.
